title: A Comprehensive Survey of Prompt Engineering Techniques in Large Language Models
author: Tonmoy Debnath, Md Nurul Absar Siddiky, Muhammad Enayetur Rahman, Prosenjit Das, Antu Kumar Guha, Muhammad Rezaur Rahman, H. M. Dipu Kabir
organization: Old Dominion University, UNC Charlotte, Khulna University, Charles Sturt University
extraction_notes: Comprehensive survey covering fundamental and advanced prompting techniques. Categorized by application domain. Includes taxonomy diagram. Covers zero-shot, few-shot, CoT variants, RAG, self-consistency, emotion prompting, code prompting, and optimization techniques. Emphasizes prompt sensitivity and hallucination reduction.
key_claims: Prompt engineering is empirical with no standardized framework for designing effective prompts
Well-structured and context-rich prompts help AI generate more accurate, relevant, and detailed responses
Many prompting techniques are susceptible to subtle variations in wording
Chain-of-Symbol prompting boosts ChatGPT accuracy from 31.8% to 92.6% on Brick World tasks with 65.8% token reduction
Thread of Thought shows 47.20% improvement in chaotic contexts for question answering
Chain-of-Table improves tabular reasoning by 8.69% on TabFact and 6.72% on WikiTQ
Logic-of-Thought improves CoT accuracy on ReClor by 4.35%
